title: RagaJSON Recording Schema
description: Machine-readable schema for cataloguing recorded performances of Indian classical music. One document describes one published video; segments reference ragas and talas by their canonical names.

source.description: Canonical URL of the video — youtube.com watch URL with the bare video id and no extra query parameters.

artist.description: Main performing artist, full name with the customary honorific (e.g. Pandit Bhimsen Joshi, Ustad Shahid Parvez).

year.description: Year of the performance, when known. Not the release or upload year — omit if only those are known.

notes.description: Free-form prose about the video as a whole: venue, accompanists, album, occasion.

segments.description: Performances contained in the video, in order of appearance. A single-performance video has one segment.

segment.title: Segment
segment.description: One performance within the video. References at least a raga or a tala: an unmetered alap has a raga and no tala, a drum solo has talas and no raga, a typical performance has both.

raga.description: Canonical name of the raga performed (as in the raga dataset).

talas.description: Canonical names of the talas used, in order of appearance (a full khyal commonly moves from a vilambit tala to a drut one). Only when attested by the source or confidently identified.

instrument.description: Solo instrument, for instrumental segments; vocal forms omit it.

start.description: Offset of the segment start within the video, in seconds. Omit for single-segment videos starting at the beginning.

notes.description: Free-form prose about this segment: bandish, tempo course, notable moments.

oneOf.description: The dominant vocal form of modern Hindustani music
The oldest surviving vocal form, austere and unhurried
Dhrupad-family form in dhamar tala, associated with Holi
Virtuosic ornamental form of Punjabi origin
Vocal form built on rhythmic syllables

instrument_enum.title: InstrumentEnum
instrument_enum.description: Solo instruments of Hindustani performance practice. The dictionary keeps transliteration consistent and grows by pull request when a new instrument is needed.
